note: 11 new leads across 5 pipelines (pr-marketing, fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, ai-consulting). Fund Formation is the hottest category today — 5 emerging managers/spinouts actively raising (Dolomite, Fedaia, Eyre Street, Air Street, Ferghana). Wealth.com closed a $65M Series B yesterday (April 16) — freshest trigger on the board. OpenArc is a $129B RIA breakaway still in active Merrill lawsuit = strong Coaching/Ops trigger. Neon DB unreachable from sandbox; run `node scripts/import-2026-04-17.cjs` locally to import.

leads.name: Wealth.com
leads.notes: Raised $65M Series B yesterday (April 16, 2026) led by Titanium Ventures with existing investors Charles Schwab, GV, Citi Ventures. 3x annual revenue growth, 664% YoY growth in AI-powered workflows. Just signed approvals from the three largest broker-dealers (50,000+ advisors) and agreements with three of top five US banks. Classic post-raise PR amplification opportunity — fresh funding story, massive enterprise wins, Ester Intelligence AI product to market.

leads.name: OpenArc Corporate Advisory
leads.fundingStage: Dynasty Financial Partners minority investor; Charles Schwab custodian
leads.notes: $129B RIA breakaway from Merrill Lynch (launched Sept 2025 on Dynasty Network). Ongoing Merrill lawsuit alleging 'pre-meditated corporate raid' — still actively defending. New national RIA = major ops buildout needed (compliance, technology, client onboarding, new-firm branding). Four managing partners split from Merrill's Global Corporate and Institutional Advisory Services unit. Shirl Penney (Dynasty CEO) called it a 'watershed moment' for RIAs.

contacts.name: Erik Bjerke
contacts.title: Senior Managing Partner (ex-Merrill, led Global Corporate & Institutional Advisory)

leads.name: Dolomite Capital
leads.fundingStage: Launched Jan 2026; $1.1B assumed from prior vintages + $200M seed for new evergreen fund
leads.source: Private Equity Wire / Bloomberg / With Intelligence (Jan 2026)
leads.notes: Taconic Capital Advisors' European credit team spun out as Dolomite Capital in January 2026. Led by Keith Magliana (MP & CIO, ran Taconic Euro credit since 2011). Took over management of $1.1B across three vintages of European Credit Dislocation Funds. Secured $200M seed from Stable Asset Management for new evergreen credit fund. New entity = new fund docs, new GP/LP structure, new compliance regime — high-value fund formation work.

contacts.name: Keith Magliana
contacts.title: Managing Partner & CIO (ex-Taconic, led Euro credit since 2011)

leads.name: depthfirst
leads.industry: AI Security / Applied AI / Enterprise Infrastructure
leads.size: Early growth ($120M total raised across 90 days)
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($80M, March 31, 2026) — 90 days after emerging from stealth with $40M Series A
leads.notes: Applied AI lab building autonomous security. $80M Series B led by Meritech just 90 days after emerging from stealth — extreme velocity signal. Founders from DeepMind, Databricks (Qasim Mithani ex-Databricks enterprise platform lead), Faire. Rapidly scaling to serve regulated enterprises = governance, model risk, AI policy needs. Adjacent to AI consulting on autonomous agents + model risk management.

leads.name: Eyre Street Capital
leads.size: Spinout (Fund I closed at $421M; Fund II targeting $800M)
leads.fundingStage: SSF II launched Oct 2025, targeting up to $800M
leads.source: PR Newswire / Morningstar / Private Equity International
leads.notes: Spin-out from Avenue Capital's impact lending platform (April 2025). Backed by Galway Sustainable Capital (with Macquarie Green Investment Group + Aware Super). SSF I was $421M; SSF II targets up to $800M (2x scale). Institutional backing + track record + large fund target = material legal/fund formation work (doubling fund size typically triggers amendments to LPA, new side letters, revised governance).

leads.name: Fedaia Partners
leads.notes: Founded 2020 by ex-Deutsche Bank Distressed Products duo Pietro Stella & Philipp Roever. Actively raising €250M debut commingled fund for asset-based special situations in Europe. Team now 8 including COO (Naumaan Amjed ex-Goldman/Fidera) and Head of IR (Zain Pirani ex-Ares). Fund I emerging manager = textbook fund formation ICP.

leads.name: Air Street Capital
leads.fundingStage: Fund III closed at $232M (March 23, 2026) — prior $17M Fund I, $121M Fund II
leads.notes: Nathan Benaich's Air Street Capital closed $232M Fund III (precisely $232,323,232) in March 2026 — now Europe's largest solo GP firm. Backs AI-first companies with $500K-$15M early-stage + up to $25M growth. Portfolio includes Synthesia ($150M ARR), Black Forest Labs, Poolside. Fund III close = LP docs/side letters/new fund structure recently completed; likely ongoing legal retainer need. Strategic fit: solo GP scaling into growth stage = new strategy needs new fund.

leads.name: Summize
leads.size: ~100+ employees (59% headcount growth in past year)
leads.notes: Manchester-based CLM with 5 consecutive years of 100%+ ARR growth. 92% bookings growth July-Dec 2025; 97% YoY ARR. Customers include Revolut, Groq, AMC Networks, NBA/MLB/NFL teams. $50M growth investment for global expansion = classic post-raise PR story (growth metrics, marquee customers, new agentic CLM launch). Opened new Manchester HQ. CEO Tom Dunlop is active publicly.

leads.name: Tazapay
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($36M total, extension led by Circle Ventures, March 2026)
leads.source: PR Newswire / FinTech Global / The Block / TechNode (March 2026)
leads.notes: Series B extension closed in March 2026 led by Circle Ventures (new: CMT Digital, Coinbase Ventures). 3 consecutive years of revenue doubling. Expanding licensing across APAC, LatAm, Middle East, and Americas — multi-region rollout = clear PR/launch story per region. Enterprise B2B fintech sweet spot for PR. Rahul Shinghal (CEO) and ex-Microsoft/Amazon/Grab team.

leads.name: Orbital
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($60M / £44M, January 2026, led by Brighton Park Capital)
leads.source: Tech.eu / Commercial Observer / Legal IT Insider / Orbital Press (January 2026)
leads.notes: UK's market leader in real estate legal AI, aggressively expanding into US post-Series B. US expansion = heavy legal consulting need: entity formation, state-by-state real estate practice rules, unauthorized practice of law (UPL) reviews, title company compliance. Board seat for Kevin Magan (Brighton Park). Serves law firms, in-house legal teams, title companies, REITs.

leads.name: Ferghana Investment Partners
leads.fundingStage: Fund I targeting $500M; backed by GP seeder New Catalyst Strategic Partners (Feb 2026)
leads.notes: Debut fund targeting $500M with strategic backing from New Catalyst Strategic Partners (GP seeder) secured in February 2026. Large target ($500M) for a Fund I = meaningful legal complexity (LPA, side letters, co-invest vehicles). Less public information so requires outreach; lower intent score because specifics are thin.
